_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD / DNB equivalent (in General medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_indications: Septic arthritis due to bacterial infection (destructive form of acute arthritis)

clinical_thresholds.note: WBC count findings in synovial joint fluids

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Did the synovial joint fluids show WBC count findings that exceeds 50,000/μL?

common_queries: Synovial fluid WBC count not documented or documented below the 50,000/μL threshold without justification
Missing synovial fluid analysis report at pre-authorization
Missing detailed indoor case papers at claim submission
Missing detailed discharge summary at claim submission

clinical_key_pointers: Septic arthritis is synonymous with an infection in a joint.
Septic arthritis is usually caused by bacteria but can also be caused by other microorganisms.
Septic arthritis due to bacterial infection is often a destructive form of acute arthritis.
Septic arthritis is a serious type of joint infection. It should be treated as soon as possible.
